coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy 6–7 miles, Z2 (130–145 bpm), rolling terrain, no hard efforts. You're 79 days out and in week 23 of a 22-week plan—you've compressed your peak. Your resting HR is 57 bpm and recovery is maxed at 100/100, which tells me you're fresh but also means you're in active taper. Wednesday is your vert day in Build/Peak phases, but right now your job is to hold fitness without adding load. Keep it conversational; if you hit 150+ bpm, dial back. The alpine will wait.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: You logged 9.5 hours of sleep last night—excellent—which means your glycogen and hormonal baseline are solid. Eat normally today: carb-forward breakfast, protein at lunch, real food at dinner. No need to carb-load yet; you're 11 weeks out. In a taper, the temptation is to underfuel because volume drops. Don't. Maintain 5.5–6 g carbs per kg bodyweight daily to keep your aerobic engine tuned and your mood stable during the mental grind of reduced training.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Your sleep efficiency is 93.3% and you're sleeping 9.5 hours—you're doing the hardest part right. Continue this rhythm: consistent bedtime, cool room, no screens 30 min before sleep. You're traveling constantly for work; that sleep debt is your biggest leak, not ice baths. Prioritize 8–9 hours per night for the next 11 weeks. One night of 6.5 hours during a business trip will cost you more fitness than one easy workout will build.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're in taper purgatory: fitter than ever, but doing less. This is where the mind games start—doubt, impatience, phantom pains. Your 5-mile run on Sunday was solid; your 12-miler last week proved you're ready. Trust the plan. The Julian Alps 50K is a long game; conserving nervous system energy now is as important as every hard interval you did in June. Use the next 11 weeks to dial in race-specific nutrition, terrain recognition (watch videos of Kranjska Gora), and pacing discipline.
